differences: The mask toolbar is now a compact direct-result Brush control and no longer blocks a large central band of the image.
The optional purple guide is off by default, leaving the processed video unobscured while painting.
Selects, range fills, and top-bar controls now share the same native-dark visual language instead of showing Aqua-style widgets.
The toolbar remains fully visible in one row at the 1440 by 900 reference viewport.
Direct Effect painting is immediately legible against the full-original frame without requiring a separate placement interpretation.
At 480px, the brush toolbar and More menu fit and remain clickable.
The narrow top bar now keeps Open, Undo, Redo, Reset, Mask, and every applicable export action visible while collapsing secondary controls.
At 800 by 500, the mask toolbar remains a single compact row and the canvas, transport, presets, and settings retain usable space.
suggestions: Retain the current primary-action priority order at narrow widths.
Keep the More menu transient because it necessarily overlays a small portion of the image on phone-width layouts.
reasoning: The supplied problem areas are resolved across desktop, native-minimum, narrow, and short-window screenshots. Range feedback is visible, Mask no longer obscures the image by default, direct brush intent is legible, and all primary actions remain reachable without horizontal overflow.
verified: Direct Original and Effect painting, temporary opposite brush, guide toggle, More-menu keyboard behavior, mask retention, and Split suppression.
Shared settings preservation, renderer-minimum size mapping, paused Flow restoration, and Reset-then-Undo restoration of export choices.
Compare and Split keyboard semantics, media labels and seek announcements, ASCII validation, custom palette controls, focus rings, and cancellable export progress.
1080p masked video held 30 to 31 fps at Floyd 4px, 28 fps at Floyd 1px, and 30 fps on the GPU fast path.
114 automated tests, all JavaScript syntax checks, git diff whitespace validation, native universal build, code-sign verification, and native PNG/GIF exports passed.
